overall_summary: Big gap to reference, but the data shows exactly where it is hiding. Attack these points. Current gap: 141.602s slower than reference. The biggest opportunity is Sector 1 (+133.762s), where 82.8 km/h is being left on the table through the corners. Executing these fixes could recover 2.46s — a completely different lap time.

priority_actions.issue: Apex speed is 51.3 km/h below reference (43.1 vs 94.4 km/h). Significant corner exit speed deficit.
priority_actions.instruction: Despite a late brake point, apex speed is below reference — car not rotating. Use higher initial brake pressure to pitch the car in, trail off through apex. Hit 94 km/h and the exit speed takes care of itself.
priority_actions.evidence: Apex: 43.1 km/h vs 94.4 km/h reference (-51.3 km/h).

priority_actions.issue: Apex speed is 28.6 km/h below reference (36.9 vs 65.5 km/h). Significant corner exit speed deficit.
priority_actions.instruction: Despite a late brake point, apex speed is below reference — car not rotating. Use higher initial brake pressure to pitch the car in, trail off through apex. Hit 66 km/h and the exit speed takes care of itself.
priority_actions.evidence: Apex: 36.9 km/h vs 65.5 km/h reference (-28.6 km/h).

priority_actions.issue: Throttle pick-up is 15m later than reference after the apex.
priority_actions.instruction: Get back to full throttle 15m earlier. Reference applies throttle at 2710m, driver at 2725m. Trust the apex and commit — late throttle bleeds straight-line speed.

sector_feedback.headline: Minimum corner speed is 82.8 km/h below reference — driver is over-slowing significantly through this sector.
sector_feedback.details: Average throttle application is 13% lower than reference — driver is hesitant getting back on power.
